name: session-memory
description: Manages cross-session learning and memory persistence. Use when user mentions 前回何をした, 履歴, 過去の作業, セッション記録, continue from before, session history. Do NOT load for: 実装作業, レビュー, 一時的な情報.
capabilities: Stores session logs with completed tasks and file changes
Records architectural decisions with rationale in decisions.md
Captures project patterns and conventions in patterns.md
author: Chachamaru127
securitySummary: Pure documentation-based skill with no executable code. All patterns detected by static scanner are false positives: markdown code formatting backticks (not shell execution), legitimate metadata fields, and documentation examples. Original audit confirmed no network calls, command execution, or environment access.
valueStatement: Context loss between sessions wastes time re-explaining project history. This skill automatically records decisions, patterns, and task progress to enable seamless continuation of work across Claude sessions.
limitations: Requires Claude Code with hooks/Stop integration for automatic saving
Does not sync across different machines or users
Manual cleanup needed when logs become too large

useCases.title: Continue multi-day projects
useCases.description: Resume work on complex features across multiple sessions without re-explaining context

useCases.title: Maintain decision records
useCases.description: Document technical choices and their rationale for team reference and onboarding

useCases.title: Track client project history
useCases.description: Keep track of client project details and decisions between different work sessions

promptTemplates.title: Continue previous work
promptTemplates.prompt: What did we work on in the last session? What tasks are remaining?

promptTemplates.title: Review decisions
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the important decisions made for this project and why.

promptTemplates.title: Learn project patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: What patterns and conventions should I follow when writing code for this project?

promptTemplates.title: Get project overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me a summary of this project including tech stack and current phase.

bestPractices: Use descriptive titles and tags in decisions.md for easy searching
Keep session-log.md focused on actionable items rather than full transcripts
Review and archive old sessions periodically to prevent file bloat
antiPatterns: Storing sensitive credentials or secrets in memory files
Committing session-log.md to version control (it contains temporary details)
Skipping decision documentation when making architectural choices

faq.question: Which Claude tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Claude Code is the primary tool. Works with Claude and Codex when hooks/Stop is configured.

faq.question: How much history can be stored?
faq.answer: No hard limit but files over 10MB may slow context loading. Manual cleanup recommended.

faq.question: Can I share memory between team members?
faq.answer: decisions.md and patterns.md should be shared. session-log.md and context.json should stay local.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Data stays local in .claude/memory/. No network calls or external access by design.

faq.question: Memory files are too large
faq.answer: Run cleanup with '整理して' command or manually remove old session entries from session-log.md.

faq.question: How is this different from global memory?
faq.answer: Session memory is project-specific. Global memory spans all projects. Use both together.
